title: Bitbucket Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Bitbucket Cloud** is Atlassian's hosted git platform exposing a REST API under `https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0`. This connector — `BitbucketConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "bitbucket"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Bitbucket Cloud workspace:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| User | `/user` | Authenticated principal (health probe) |
| Workspaces | `/workspaces` | List + read workspaces |
| Repositories | `/repositories/{ws}` | List, read, create, delete repos |
| Branches | `/repositories/{ws}/{slug}/refs/branches` | List branches |
| Pull Requests | `/repositories/{ws}/{slug}/pullrequests` | List, get, create, merge PRs |
| Issues | `/repositories/{ws}/{slug}/issues` | List, get, create issues |
| Commits | `/repositories/{ws}/{slug}/commits` | List + read commits (optionally per-branch) |
| Snippets | `/snippets/{ws}` | Workspace snippets |
| Webhooks | `/repositories/{ws}/{slug}/hooks` | List + create webhooks |
| Files | `/repositories/{ws}/{slug}/src/{commit}/{path}` | Raw file content |

The connector normalises repositories + pull requests + issues into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + Retry-After (3 attempts), refreshes the OAuth token on a 401 (one-shot replay), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::BitbucketHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Create a Bitbucket OAuth consumer

1. Sign in to https://bitbucket.org.
2. Open **Workspace settings → OAuth consumers** (you must own or admin the workspace).
3. Click **Add consumer**:
   - **Name**: `Shielva ACP`
   - **Callback URL**: paste the redirect URI your ACP gateway exposes (e.g. `https://acp.shielva.example/oauth/bitbucket/callback`).
   - **This is a private consumer**: leave checked.
   - **Permissions**: tick `Account`, `Repositories: Read/Write`, `Pull requests: Read/Write`, `Issues: Read/Write`.
4. Save. Bitbucket displays the **Key** and **Secret** — copy both immediately.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Bitbucket**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` (string) | OAuth consumer **Key** |
| `client_secret` (secret) | OAuth consumer **Secret** |
| `scopes` (string, optional) | Leave default unless your consumer was scoped narrower |
| `auth_url` / `token_url` / `base_url` (optional) | Leave blank to use Bitbucket Cloud defaults |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates that `client_id` + `client_secret` are present (it does NOT call the API). The gateway will then redirect the user through Bitbucket's authorize URL and call `authorize(auth_code)` on the redirect.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()`, which calls `GET /user` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → in-client refresh + replay; second 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `DEGRADED + AUTHENTICATED` (the token still authenticates but lacks the requested scope). On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Bitbucket Cloud uses **OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant** (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`). Access tokens expire after 2 hours; refresh tokens are minted at first authorize.

### Credential model

- `client_id` / `client_secret` — the Bitbucket OAuth consumer **Key** + **Secret** (per-tenant, persisted in encrypted ACP config).
- `redirect_uri` — the ACP gateway's callback URL, injected into `self.config` before `authorize()` runs.
- Access token sent in `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` on every API request.
- Token endpoint requests use **HTTP Basic** with `(client_id, client_secret)` and `Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `client_id` + `client_secret`. Persists redirect_uri. Returns `(HEALTHY, PENDING)`. Does NOT call the API. |
| `authorize(auth_code, state)` | POST `grant_type=authorization_code` → `TOKEN_URI`. Stores access + refresh via `set_token`. |
| `on_token_refresh()` | POST `grant_type=refresh_token` ahead of expiry (`BaseConnector.ensure_token()` triggers). Preserves the prior refresh_token if Bitbucket omits a new one. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /user`. |
| 401 mid-flight | In-client one-shot refresh via the `token_refresh` callback wired in `__init__`. Replay the original request. A second 401 surfaces `BitbucketAuthError`. |

### Header contract

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json   (write requests only)
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | string | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth consumer Key |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth consumer Secret |
| `scopes` | string | no | `self.scopes` | Space-separated OAuth scopes |
| `auth_url` | string | no | `self.auth_url` (default `https://bitbucket.org/site/oauth2/authorize`) | Override only for proxy/sandbox |
| `token_url` | string | no | `self.token_url` (default `https://bitbucket.org/site/oauth2/access_token`) | Override only for proxy/sandbox |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (default `https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0`) | Override only for proxy/sandbox |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |
| `redirect_uri` | string | no (gateway-injected) | `self.redirect_uri` | OAuth callback URL — required at code exchange |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "client_secret"]
REQUIRED_SCOPES = [
    "account", "repository", "repository:write",
    "pullrequest", "pullrequest:write",
    "issue", "issue:write",
]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED", "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("DEGRADED", "AUTHENTICATED"),
    429: ("DEGRADED", "CONNECTED"),
}
_BITBUCKET_BASE = "https://api.bitbucket.org/2.0"
AUTH_URI  = "https://bitbucket.org/site/oauth2/authorize"
TOKEN_URI = "https://bitbucket.org/site/oauth2/access_token"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `BitbucketConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::BitbucketHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `client_id` + `client_secret`. Returns `(HEALTHY, PENDING)` with `metadata.requires_oauth_redirect=True`. Does NOT call the Bitbucket API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
POST `grant_type=authorization_code` to `TOKEN_URI` with HTTP Basic `(client_id, client_secret)`. Persists access + refresh token.

### `async on_token_refresh() -> TokenInfo`
Called by `BaseConnector.ensure_token()` ahead of expiry. POST `grant_type=refresh_token` with HTTP Basic. Preserves the existing refresh_token if the response omits a new one.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
`GET /user`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates `workspaces → repositories → open pull requests` and emits one `NormalizedDocument` per PR via `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Workspaces & Repositories
children.content:
### `async get_current_user() -> Dict`
`GET /user` — raw response.

### `async list_workspaces(*, role="member", pagelen=50) -> Dict`
`GET /workspaces`.

### `async get_workspace(workspace) -> Dict`
`GET /workspaces/{workspace}`.

### `async list_repositories(workspace, *, role="member", pagelen=50, page=1) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{workspace}` — page-based pagination via `page` + `pagelen`.

### `async get_repository(workspace, repo_slug) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{workspace}/{repo_slug}`.

### `async create_repository(workspace, repo_slug, *, scm="git", is_private=True, description="", project_key=None) -> Dict`
`POST /repositories/{workspace}/{repo_slug}`.

### `async delete_repository(workspace, repo_slug) -> Dict`
`DELETE /repositories/{workspace}/{repo_slug}` — returns `{}` on 204.

children.title: Branches & Commits
children.content:
### `async list_branches(workspace, repo_slug, *, pagelen=50) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/refs/branches`.

### `async list_commits(workspace, repo_slug, *, branch=None, pagelen=50) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/commits[/{branch}]`.

### `async get_commit(workspace, repo_slug, commit) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/commit/{node}`.

### `async get_file_content(workspace, repo_slug, commit, path) -> str`
`GET /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/src/{commit}/{path}` — returns raw text.

children.title: Pull Requests
children.content:
### `async list_pull_requests(workspace, repo_slug, *, state="OPEN", pagelen=50) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/pullrequests`.

### `async get_pull_request(workspace, repo_slug, pull_id) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/pullrequests/{id}`.

### `async create_pull_request(workspace, repo_slug, title, source_branch, destination_branch="main", description="", reviewers=None) -> Dict`
`POST /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/pullrequests`. `reviewers` is a list of UUIDs (with surrounding braces) or account_ids; each becomes `{uuid: ...}` in the body.

### `async merge_pull_request(workspace, repo_slug, pull_id, *, merge_strategy="merge_commit", message=None) -> Dict`
`POST /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/pullrequests/{id}/merge`. Strategy is `merge_commit | squash | fast_forward`.

children.title: Issues
children.content:
### `async list_issues(workspace, repo_slug, *, state="new", pagelen=50) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/issues` — state filter via `?q=state="new"`.

### `async get_issue(workspace, repo_slug, issue_id) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/issues/{id}`.

### `async create_issue(workspace, repo_slug, title, *, content="", priority="minor", kind="bug") -> Dict`
`POST /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/issues`. The body's content envelope is `{raw: ...}`.

children.title: Webhooks
children.content:
### `async list_webhooks(workspace, repo_slug) -> Dict`
`GET /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/hooks`.

### `async create_webhook(workspace, repo_slug, description, url, *, events=None, active=True) -> Dict`
`POST /repositories/{ws}/{slug}/hooks`. `events` defaults to `["repo:push"]`. Common values: `repo:push`, `pullrequest:created`, `pullrequest:updated`, `pullrequest:fulfilled`, `pullrequest:rejected`, `issue:created`, `issue:updated`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
BitbucketError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── BitbucketAuthError                   # 401 / 403
├── BitbucketBadRequestError             # 400
├── BitbucketNotFoundError               # 404
├── BitbucketConflictError               # 409
├── BitbucketRateLimitError              # 429 — retry_after_s
└── BitbucketServerError                 # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
BitbucketNetworkError = BitbucketServerError
BitbucketNotFound     = BitbucketNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 401 | Single-shot in-client refresh via `_token_refresh` callback; replay; second 401 → `BitbucketAuthError` |
| 403 | Raise `BitbucketAuthError` immediately (scope issue, refresh won't help) |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After`; exponential backoff `_BASE_DELAY_S * _BACKOFF_FACTOR^attempt + jitter` capped at `_MAX_DELAY_S=32`; up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff + retry, then `BitbucketNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, AUTHENTICATED)
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
other → _classify_failure(exc) → DEGRADED + FAILED
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `client_id` or `client_secret` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. The OAuth consumer Key + Secret are in **Workspace settings → OAuth consumers**. |
| `BitbucketAuthError: Token exchange failed: HTTP 400 invalid_grant` at authorize | The OAuth `redirect_uri` differs from the one registered on the consumer | Make sure the ACP gateway's callback URL is byte-identical to the **Callback URL** field on the consumer. |
| `BitbucketAuthError: ... HTTP 401` after refresh | Refresh token revoked (user signed out, consumer rotated) | Re-run the OAuth flow from the gateway. The connector clears its token on `disconnect()`. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, AUTHENTICATED)` on health_check | 403 — token authenticates but lacks scope (e.g. `pullrequest:write`) | Edit the OAuth consumer permissions in Bitbucket, then re-authorize to mint a token with the broader scopes. |
| `bitbucket.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (per-hour rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff + Retry-After. Reduce `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer concurrent requests. |
| 404 on `get_repository(workspace, repo_slug)` with what looks like the right slug | The slug is the URL slug (kebab-case) — not the repository display name. Use `repo.slug` from `list_repositories`, not `repo.name`. | Inspect a repo dict; `slug` is canonical. |
| 409 on `merge_pull_request` | The PR is already MERGED or has unresolved conflicts | Refresh the PR with `get_pull_request`; if `state == "MERGED"` the merge has already landed. |
| `BitbucketRateLimitError` raised even though `with_retry` is set | The HTTP client exhausted its retry budget (`_MAX_RETRIES=3`); `with_retry` re-tries again but Bitbucket is still throttling | Wait for the window to reset (Bitbucket caps per IP + per principal, typically 60 min) or reduce concurrency. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'BitbucketNetworkError'` | Older code expecting the old name | Already covered — `BitbucketNetworkError = BitbucketServerError` alias is in `exceptions.py`. |
| Webhook signature mismatch on Bitbucket-delivered events | Custom HMAC verification mismatch | Bitbucket's hook deliveries don't include an X-Hub-Signature by default; if you registered a shared secret, HMAC the raw body with SHA-256. Double-check the secret in **Repository settings → Webhooks**. |
